Color |
faq-0000a05-shared | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: What security measures does Epson recommend for its network products?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: To help deter unauthorized access to your product over a network, you should protect your network environment using appropriate security measures. Security measures such as these can help deter threats such as loss of user data, use of telephone and fax lines, and other intrusions: Enable security on your wireless LAN if your product supports a wireless connection Enable the appropriate security on the wireless LAN you plan to use with your product. Network security, such as a network password, can deter interception of traffic over the wireless LAN. Your router may already have a default password enabled by your Internet service provider (ISP). See your ISP and router documentation for instructions on how to change the default password and better secure your network. Connect your product only to a network protected by a firewall Connecting your product directly to the Internet may leave it vulnerable to security threats. Instead, connect it to a router or other networking device protected by a firewall. Your router may already have a firewall set up by your Internet service provider; check with your ISP for confirmation. For best results, set up and use a private IP address for your network connection. Change the default administrator password on your product If your product has an option to set an administrator password, change the default administrator password to deter access by unauthorized users to personal data stored on your product, such as IDs, passwords, and contact lists. See the product's User's Guide for more information.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-00004c5-shared | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: I see the message Filter Failure with rastertoescpII when I try to print from my Mac. What should I do?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Do the following: On your Mac, open the menu, select System Preferences or System Settings, and then select Sharing. Do one of the following: If there is no name in the Computer Name field, enter a name for your computer. If there is a name in the Computer Name field, rename your computer. Close the window. Open the menu, select System Preferences or System Settings > Print & Scan, Print & Fax, or Printers & Scanners > [your printer name] > Options & Supplies. Select General, if necessary. Enter your Computer Name as the Location and click OK. Try printing again. If you can print now, you're done. If you can't print, go to the next step. Turn off your product. Open the menu, select System Preferences or System Settings > Print & Scan, Print & Fax, or Printers & Scanners > [your printer name] and click . Click Delete Printer and close the window. Open your hard drive and do each of the following: Select Library > Printers and drag the Epson folder to the trash. Select Library > Printers > PPDs > Contents > Resources and drag the Epson Printer Name.gz file to the trash. Select Library > Caches and drag the Epson folder to the trash. Close the hard drive and empty the trash on your Mac. Restart your Mac, turn on your product, and then reinstall the driver for your product. Try to print again. If this does not solve the problem, contact Epson Support.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-00009e6-shared | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: How do I save my Epson printer driver presets so I can restore them if I reinstall the Epson printer driver in Windows?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Note: You may lose some custom settings when you uninstall the printer driver. Before you reinstall the printer driver, do the following: Open File Explorer or Windows Explorer. Select This PC or Computer > C: drive > Program Files > Epson > [your printer name]. Move the UCI extension files to a different folder on your desktop. Note: After you reinstall the Epson printer driver, move the UCI extension files back to your printer's folder as described in step 2.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000a7a-shared | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Can I use the printer without the waste ink bottle installed?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: No, the waste ink bottle must be installed at all times to capture waste ink, which is a by-product of printing. Make sure the waste ink bottle is installed even when the printer is not powered on.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-00009ea-shared | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: I see the message Communication Error when I use a USB connection. What should I do?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Do the following: Delete all print jobs in the print queue. Turn the printer off. Note: Make sure the USB cable is connected correctly. Restart your computer. When the desktop appears, turn your printer on and follow the on-screen instructions. Note: If the problem continues, turn the printer off again and delete the printer in the list of printers. Then disconnect the USB cable from the printer and connect it again.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-00009ed-shared | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Where are the ICC profiles located for my printer on my Mac?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Do the following to locate the files: Open your hard drive, then select Library > Printers > EPSON > InkjetPrinter2 > ICCProfiles. Press and hold the Control key on your keyboard while you click the profile package in the ICCProfiles folder. In the pop-up menu that appears, select Show Package Contents. Select Contents > Resources.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-00009e4-shared | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: How do I determine my printer driver version on my Mac?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Open the menu and select System Preferences or System Settings > Printers & Scan, Print & Fax, or Printers & Scanners > [your printer name] > Options & Supplies. Your printer driver version is displayed.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-00009e9-shared | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: The colors on my printouts do not match the colors on my computer monitor. What should I do?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Try the following recommended color correction methods: Use ICC profiles during the color management process. Ensure that you are using the correct output profile for your loaded media. Note: You may need to create a custom ICC profile for the best results. Ensure that your monitor is calibrated and is displaying accurate color. Compare the print result with the image on your computer screen in a well-lit environment. Since monitors and printers produce colors differently, printed colors and screen colors do not always look the same. If the recommendations above do not help, contact Epson support. The color management process is complex. We can assist you with the proper assignment of settings; however, we cannot assist you with creating a custom color profile.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-00004db-shared | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Who do I contact for software support?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Click on the Contact Us tab on your product's support page to contact Epson for support. If your product comes with third-party software, contact the software company.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000e88-shared | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: I can tell some ink is still in the ink pack. Why does my printer show that the pack is expended?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: The ink pack is designed to include a small safety reserve of ink. This safety reserve prevents damage to the printhead. If the printer tries to print when no ink remains in the pack, air bubbles would enter the ink delivery system and cause damage to the printhead. The safety reserve of ink prevents this from happening.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000ae0-sc_s8170 | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Does the product support borderless printing?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: No, borderless printing is not supported.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-00004c5-shared | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: I see the message Filter Failure with rastertoescpII when I try to print from my Mac. What should I do?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Do the following: On your Mac, open the menu, select System Preferences or System Settings, and then select Sharing. Do one of the following: If there is no name in the Computer Name field, enter a name for your computer. If there is a name in the Computer Name field, rename your computer. Close the window. Open the menu, select System Preferences or System Settings > Print & Scan, Print & Fax, or Printers & Scanners > [your printer name] > Options & Supplies. Select General, if necessary. Enter your Computer Name as the Location and click OK. Try printing again. If you can print now, you're done. If you can't print, go to the next step. Turn off your product. Open the menu, select System Preferences or System Settings > Print & Scan, Print & Fax, or Printers & Scanners > [your printer name] and click . Click Delete Printer and close the window. Open your hard drive and do each of the following: Select Library > Printers and drag the Epson folder to the trash. Select Library > Printers > PPDs > Contents > Resources and drag the Epson Printer Name.gz file to the trash. Select Library > Caches and drag the Epson folder to the trash. Close the hard drive and empty the trash on your Mac. Restart your Mac, turn on your product, and then reinstall the driver for your product. Try to print again. If this does not solve the problem, contact Epson Support.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-00009ea-shared | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: I see the message Communication Error when I use a USB connection. What should I do?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Do the following: Delete all print jobs in the print queue. Turn the printer off. Note: Make sure the USB cable is connected correctly. Restart your computer. When the desktop appears, turn your printer on and follow the on-screen instructions. Note: If the problem continues, turn the printer off again and delete the printer in the list of printers. Then disconnect the USB cable from the printer and connect it again.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000a7a-shared | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Can I use the printer without the waste ink bottle installed?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: No, the waste ink bottle must be installed at all times to capture waste ink, which is a by-product of printing. Make sure the waste ink bottle is installed even when the printer is not powered on.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000e88-shared | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: I can tell some ink is still in the ink pack. Why does my printer show that the pack is expended?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: The ink pack is designed to include a small safety reserve of ink. This safety reserve prevents damage to the printhead. If the printer tries to print when no ink remains in the pack, air bubbles would enter the ink delivery system and cause damage to the printhead. The safety reserve of ink prevents this from happening.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000b93-sc_s8170 | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: What size ink packs are available?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Ink packs are available in 800 ml and 1500 ml sizes.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000adb-sc_s8170 | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Which type of ink does the product use?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: The product uses Epson UltraChrome GS3 ink.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000ade-sc_s8170 | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: How many ink colors does the product use?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: The printer has a 6-color ink set. It uses the colors listed below. Black Cyan Magenta Yellow Light Cyan Light Magenta

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000c88-sc_s8170 | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Can the product automatically detect when a nozzle is clogged?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: The printer is equipped with Nozzle Verification Technology which monitors for clogged nozzles. If the set threshold of clogged nozzles has been exceeded, the printer will perform an automatic print head cleaning. Note: The Nozzle Verification Technology feature may not detect all clogged nozzles. A small amount of ink may be consumed when the printer checks for clogged nozzles. The printer may periodically perform cleanings automatically to maintain print quality.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000612-sc_s8170 | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: What media thickness does the printer accept?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: The product supports the following media thickness: Roll paper: 1 mm (0.03 in.) or less

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000c05-sc_s8170 | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Does the product support roll and cut sheet media?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: The printer supports only roll media.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000c02-sc_s8170 | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Which roll media core sizes can I use with my product?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: You can use roll media with a 2-inch (51 mm) or 3-inch (76.2 mm) core.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-00009e9-shared | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: The colors on my printouts do not match the colors on my computer monitor. What should I do?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Try the following recommended color correction methods: Use ICC profiles during the color management process. Ensure that you are using the correct output profile for your loaded media. Note: You may need to create a custom ICC profile for the best results. Ensure that your monitor is calibrated and is displaying accurate color. Compare the print result with the image on your computer screen in a well-lit environment. Since monitors and printers produce colors differently, printed colors and screen colors do not always look the same. If the recommendations above do not help, contact Epson support. The color management process is complex. We can assist you with the proper assignment of settings; however, we cannot assist you with creating a custom color profile.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000c77-sc_s8170 | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: I see horizontal or vertical lines in my printout. What should I do?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Perform a nozzle check from the printer's control panel, then do one of the following: If there are gaps in the nozzle check pattern, perform a print head cleaning. If there are no gaps in the nozzle check pattern, try the solutions below: Horizontal Banding Select the correct media type in the media settings using the printer's control panel. Perform a print adjustment in the media settings using the printer's control panel. Vertical Banding Perform a print quality adjustment using the printer's control panel.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-00009e4-shared | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: How do I determine my printer driver version on my Mac?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Open the menu and select System Preferences or System Settings > Printers & Scan, Print & Fax, or Printers & Scanners > [your printer name] > Options & Supplies. Your printer driver version is displayed.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-00004db-shared | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Who do I contact for software support?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Click on the Contact Us tab on your product's support page to contact Epson for support. If your product comes with third-party software, contact the software company.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-00009ed-shared | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Where are the ICC profiles located for my printer on my Mac?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Do the following to locate the files: Open your hard drive, then select Library > Printers > EPSON > InkjetPrinter2 > ICCProfiles. Press and hold the Control key on your keyboard while you click the profile package in the ICCProfiles folder. In the pop-up menu that appears, select Show Package Contents. Select Contents > Resources.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-00009e6-shared | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: How do I save my Epson printer driver presets so I can restore them if I reinstall the Epson printer driver in Windows?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Note: You may lose some custom settings when you uninstall the printer driver. Before you reinstall the printer driver, do the following: Open File Explorer or Windows Explorer. Select This PC or Computer > C: drive > Program Files > Epson > [your printer name]. Move the UCI extension files to a different folder on your desktop. Note: After you reinstall the Epson printer driver, move the UCI extension files back to your printer's folder as described in step 2.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-00004bc-sc_s8170 | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: How do I manually assign a static IP address to my product?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Do the following: Download and install the EpsonNet Config Utility from the Downloads tab on your product's support page. Do one of the following: Windows 11: Click , then search for EpsonNet Config and select it. Windows 10: Click and select EpsonNet > EpsonNet Config. Windows 8.x: Navigate to the Apps screen and select EpsonNet Config. Windows (other versions): Select or Start > Programs > EpsonNet > EpsonNet Config. Highlight your printer's name and click Configuration. Select Basic (under TCP/IP) and make sure Manual is selected. Enter the IP Address, Subnet Mask, and Default Gateway in the IP Address Setting section. Click Send or Transmit. Close EpsonNet Config.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000d00-sc_s8170 | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Which software is included with the product?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: The printer comes with the following: Communication Driver Epson Edge Dashboard Epson Edge Print PRO Epson Software Updater EpsonNet Config SE Web Config ONYX Go Plus RIP (1-year subscription)

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000c06-sc_s8170 | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Which connection types does the product support?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: The product supports the following connection types: SuperSpeed USB 3.0 (1 port) 100Base-TX/1000Base-T Ethernet (1 port)

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000f4d-shared | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: How do I install a printer in Windows 11 24H2 or later?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: See our Installing a Printer in Windows 11 24H2 or Later page for details.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000a05-shared | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: What security measures does Epson recommend for its network products?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: To help deter unauthorized access to your product over a network, you should protect your network environment using appropriate security measures. Security measures such as these can help deter threats such as loss of user data, use of telephone and fax lines, and other intrusions: Enable security on your wireless LAN if your product supports a wireless connection Enable the appropriate security on the wireless LAN you plan to use with your product. Network security, such as a network password, can deter interception of traffic over the wireless LAN. Your router may already have a default password enabled by your Internet service provider (ISP). See your ISP and router documentation for instructions on how to change the default password and better secure your network. Connect your product only to a network protected by a firewall Connecting your product directly to the Internet may leave it vulnerable to security threats. Instead, connect it to a router or other networking device protected by a firewall. Your router may already have a firewall set up by your Internet service provider; check with your ISP for confirmation. For best results, set up and use a private IP address for your network connection. Change the default administrator password on your product If your product has an option to set an administrator password, change the default administrator password to deter access by unauthorized users to personal data stored on your product, such as IDs, passwords, and contact lists. See the product's User's Guide for more information.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000c71-sc_s8170 | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Does the printer have a built-in cutter?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: No, the printer does not have a built-in cutter.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000f4a-shared | SPT_SCS8170PE | Epson SureColor S8170 | SureColor Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Where do I find my product's serial number?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: See our Finding Your Epson Product Serial Number page for details.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000c1d-shared | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Does OS X support manual duplex printing with my product?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: This feature is not supported in Mac OS X. However, if your printer has a built-in automatic duplexer, auto-duplexing is supported in Mac OS X 10.6 (Snow Leopard).

coveo:metadata.title: faq-00004b0-shared | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Does Epson have a program where I can donate my working electronic equipment?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Yes. Epson America, Inc. and Digitunity have joined together with the goal of helping those who are facing economic challenges or have a disability to gain access to today's technology. With your donation, you not only help the environment, but you also help change the lives of those who need these products the most.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-000096d-shared | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Where can I get replacement parts or a service manual?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: You can purchase replacement parts and service manuals from an Epson Authorized Customer Care Center or Epson parts distributor.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-00004dc-shared | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Does Epson make drivers for Linux?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Linux drivers are available for some products. Visit the links below to see the drivers available for your product, as well as information about using an Epson product with Linux. Note: Epson does not provide support for Linux drivers. Linux drivers for Epson products Linux FAQs for Epson products(only available in English) Linux manuals for Epson products(only available in English)

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000aaf-shared | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Are the ink cartridges refillable?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: EPSON does not recommend refilling ink cartridges. Refilled cartridges can cause print quality problems. In the printer's User's Guide, we advise "Use only genuine EPSON ink cartridges and do not refill them. Other products may cause damage not covered by EPSON's warranty".

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000ab0-shared | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Calibration doesn't seem to have any effect. What should I do?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Make sure that whenever you turn off the printer, you are always using the printer's power button. Never use an external switch, such as a power strip, surge protector, or power protector, to turn off the printer. If you turn the printer off using an external switch, calibration settings will not be saved.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000f4a-shared | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Where do I find my product's serial number?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: See our Finding Your Epson Product Serial Number page for details.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-45700 | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: How do I use Epson iron-on transfers?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Using New and Improved EPSON Iron-On Cool Peel TransfersIf you've enjoyed EPSON Iron-On transfers before, you'll like these even more!Here's why:Cool peel process makes it easy for everyone in the family to share in creating iron-on projects!Improved image quality and resolution!Superior image feel and fabric texture before and after washing!With EPSON Iron-On Cool Peel Transfers, you can transfer your images and photos to T-shirts, sweatshirts, tablecloths, napkins, aprons, or just about any cotton or cotton-blend fabric. For best results, use light colored material with a smooth texture.It is available from the Epson Store or through your local computer retailer.What You'll NeedBefore you start, make sure you have these items:* EPSON Iron-On Cool Peel Transfers* Any EPSON Color ink jet printer* Flat, hard, smooth, non-porous ironing surface thatwill not be damaged by heat. Do not use an ironing board, wood, glass or metal.* Pillowcase* Item to receive transfer image (T-shirt. apron. etc.)* Hand iron or heat pressNote: If using a commercial heat press, set the temperature for 375 degrees F and press for 20 seconds.Creating the ImageYou can use any application to create the image. Just make you that you select the "Flip Horizontal" option in the EPSON stylus Printer driver so the image will be transferred correctly onto the fabric. If your image does not include any text, you may not need to reverse the image when printing it. Printing the ImageWhen you are ready to print your image, follow these steps:1. In the EPSON Stylus printer driver select "360 dpi + Ink Jet Paper" for the Media Type. (This setting will also automatically select 360 dpi for the Print Quality setting.)2. Make sure you select "Flip Horizontal" in safely to one side.Wait between one and two minutes for the transfer to cool starting with any corner. Peel the transfer sheet away from the fabric item. Removing the paper may be more difficult if it is allowed to cool for too long.Ironing a Small ImageSmall images (4" x 4" or less) are easier to transfer than larger ones.1. Start with the iron on one side of the transfer. Slowly movethe iron across the transfer for 5 seconds. This will securethe transfer onto the fabric item.2. Pressing firmly, wiggle the iron over the image, moving itconstantly for 45 seconds to 1 minute. Be sure not to scorch your fabric item.3. Set the iron safely to one side. Wait between one and tw ominutes for the transfer to cool. Starting with any corner, peel the transfer sheet away from the fabric item. Removing the paper may be more difficult if it is allowed to cool for too long.Tip If you will be applying more than one image on the fabric, make sure they are spaced far enough apart so you can iron one without touching another image that has already been transferred.Washing Your New CreationYour new EPSON transfer creation is sturdy and machinewashable, but you can protect the color and quality by following these simple washing guidelines:Wash in cold water only. If possible, use a detergent withcolor protection.If you are washing a T-shirt or other similar garment, turn it inside out before washing. Wash it by itself as the somecolors may run slightly the first time it is washed. If the colors run, they will usually wash out the second time the article is washed. Remove the article as soon as the washing machine completes its cycle. Colors may run if the article is allowed to sit before drying.Dry it in the dryer with normal settings.EPS0N and EPSON Stylus are registered trademarks of Seiko Epson Corporation Copyright (1997)* by Seiko Epson Corporation

coveo:metadata.title: faq-36919 | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: My printout has lines running through it. What should I do?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: This phenomena is called banding and may be caused by a clogged nozzle. Print the self test page by pressing the Load/Eject button while applying power to the printer. Check to see if white lines appear through your text. If the white lines are present, perform two cleaning cycles as described under the front cover of the printer; (COLOR Cleaning)-Press the PAUSE button, then ALT and Economy Condensed, or (BLACK Cleaning)-Press the PAUSE button, then ALT and Load/Eject. If the ink cartridge is new and the cleaning cycles did not correct the problem, print the default settings (Press Economy/Condensed and power the printer on) to ensure that "Autoline Feed" is off. If the "Autoline Feed" is off, and the test print still shows missing dots, have the printer sent in for repair. If the ink cartridge is old, and the cleaning cycles did not correct the problem, print the default settings to ensure that "Autoline Feed" is off. If the "Autoline Feed" is off, replace the ink cartridge. You may have to perform a few cleaning cycles after installing the new ink cartridge. Banding may also occur if the user turns the printer off by a power bar. If this is the case, the user may need to run up to 10 cleaning cycles to correct the problem. If the condition persists and it is visible in the demo page, the printer needs to be serviced. The Troubleshooting Guide located in Chapter Six of the User's Manual provides additional information.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-36920 | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: I am not getting either black or color on the self test page. What should I do?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Make sure the economy light on the printer is not on. This will ensure that the economy mode option is not selected. Using the instructions on the inside front cover of the printer as a guide, perform a black and then a color cleaning cycle; (COLOR Cleaning)-Press the PAUSE button, then ALT and Economy Condensed, or (BLACK Cleaning)-Press the PAUSE button, then ALT and Load/Eject. If this does not correct the problem, replace the appropriate ink cartridge.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000c1d-shared | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Does OS X support manual duplex printing with my product?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: This feature is not supported in Mac OS X. However, if your printer has a built-in automatic duplexer, auto-duplexing is supported in Mac OS X 10.6 (Snow Leopard).

coveo:metadata.title: faq-45694 | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Which ink cartridges should I use with my product?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Error While Adding Use the following Epson ink cartridges with the Epson Stylus Pro.Ink cartridgePart numberBlackS020034ColorS020036

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000aaf-shared | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Are the ink cartridges refillable?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Error While Adding EPSON does not recommend refilling ink cartridges. Refilled cartridges can cause print quality problems. In the printer's User's Guide, we advise "Use only genuine EPSON ink cartridges and do not refill them. Other products may cause damage not covered by EPSON's warranty".

coveo:metadata.title: faq-45700 | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: How do I use Epson iron-on transfers?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Error While Adding Using New and Improved EPSON Iron-On Cool Peel TransfersIf you've enjoyed EPSON Iron-On transfers before, you'll like these even more!Here's why:Cool peel process makes it easy for everyone in the family to share in creating iron-on projects!Improved image quality and resolution!Superior image feel and fabric texture before and after washing!With EPSON Iron-On Cool Peel Transfers, you can transfer your images and photos to T-shirts, sweatshirts, tablecloths, napkins, aprons, or just about any cotton or cotton-blend fabric. For best results, use light colored material with a smooth texture.It is available from the Epson Store or through your local computer retailer.What You'll NeedBefore you start, make sure you have these items:* EPSON Iron-On Cool Peel Transfers* Any EPSON Color ink jet printer* Flat, hard, smooth, non-porous ironing surface thatwill not be damaged by heat. Do not use an ironing board, wood, glass or metal.* Pillowcase* Item to receive transfer image (T-shirt. apron. etc.)* Hand iron or heat pressNote: If using a commercial heat press, set the temperature for 375 degrees F and press for 20 seconds.Creating the ImageYou can use any application to create the image. Just make you that you select the "Flip Horizontal" option in the EPSON stylus Printer driver so the image will be transferred correctly onto the fabric. If your image does not include any text, you may not need to reverse the image when printing it. Printing the ImageWhen you are ready to print your image, follow these steps:1. In the EPSON Stylus printer driver select "360 dpi + Ink Jet Paper" for the Media Type. (This setting will also automatically select 360 dpi for the Print Quality setting.)2. Make sure you select "Flip Horizontal" in safely to one side.Wait between one and two minutes for the transfer to cool starting with any corner. Peel the transfer sheet away from the fabric item. Removing the paper may be more difficult if it is allowed to cool for too long.Ironing a Small ImageSmall images (4" x 4" or less) are easier to transfer than larger ones.1. Start with the iron on one side of the transfer. Slowly movethe iron across the transfer for 5 seconds. This will securethe transfer onto the fabric item.2. Pressing firmly, wiggle the iron over the image, moving itconstantly for 45 seconds to 1 minute. Be sure not to scorch your fabric item.3. Set the iron safely to one side. Wait between one and tw ominutes for the transfer to cool. Starting with any corner, peel the transfer sheet away from the fabric item. Removing the paper may be more difficult if it is allowed to cool for too long.Tip If you will be applying more than one image on the fabric, make sure they are spaced far enough apart so you can iron one without touching another image that has already been transferred.Washing Your New CreationYour new EPSON transfer creation is sturdy and machinewashable, but you can protect the color and quality by following these simple washing guidelines:Wash in cold water only. If possible, use a detergent withcolor protection.If you are washing a T-shirt or other similar garment, turn it inside out before washing. Wash it by itself as the somecolors may run slightly the first time it is washed. If the colors run, they will usually wash out the second time the article is washed. Remove the article as soon as the washing machine completes its cycle. Colors may run if the article is allowed to sit before drying.Dry it in the dryer with normal settings.EPS0N and EPSON Stylus are registered trademarks of Seiko Epson Corporation Copyright (1997)* by Seiko Epson Corporation

coveo:metadata.title: faq-51851 | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: My product doesn't feed paper correctly. What should I do?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Error While Adding Here are a few suggestions to try:You can try using the Epson cleaning sheet (has a sticky side) to pick up any excess paper residue. It's available from the Epson Store.Try loading heavier stock paper, such as 24 lb. paper, if you haven't done so already.Make sure there is 1/16 to 1/32 inch space on each side of the paper in the paper tray. Move the paper type selector back and forth 5 times, to make sure it is not stuck.Feed the tray with a minimum of 20 sheets of paper and try to see if it loads paper through.The paper thickness lever has two settings, one for plain paper, (thin paper 20# to 24#) and one for envelopes and thick paper.It should be on the 0 setting for thin paper and on the + setting for thick paper and envelopes.The paper adjustment lever may be adjusted in between the two settings to accommodate paper that is not standard or that the operating/environmental conditions have changed. Make sure the paper is loaded far enough into the printer to ensure good contact between the paper and the paper feed mechanism.Reload the paperIf the printer continues to feed too many sheets, the paper may be sticking together (due to humidity)For testing purposes, try the following:1. Place 20-30 sheets of paper in a microwave oven for 3/4 seconds on the medium setting (to dry the paper)2. Fan the paper, separating each sheet to ensure that the paper is not sticking together.3. Reload the paper into the printer.4. Test print your document.If all else fails change the brand or type of paper used, the paper specifications are located in the printer's user's manual.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-36905 | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: The printed output I am getting is unacceptable. What should I do?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Error While Adding Make sure the Economy light on the printer is not on, and if printing on EPSON special coated paper, ensure that you are printing on the coated side of the paper. If the Economy light is on, press the Economy/Condensed button multiple times until the Economy/Condensed lights are both off. Economy mode is only intended for printing rough drafts and should not be enabled for normal printing. Try printing again. If printing has not improved, perform a cleaning cycle. For COLOR Cleaning press the Pause button, then push ALT and Economy/Condensed; or for BLACK Cleaning press the Pause button, then ALT and Load/Eject. Print the self test page to see if printing has improved (Power on the printer while holding down the Load/Eject button for 10 seconds or until the data light comes on or paper loads). If the quality is acceptable, verify that the correct driver is selected as the default driver. Make sure that COLOR is selected in the driver. Ensure that HALFTONING is NOT selected in the driver. You may want to manipulate the settings within the application to achieve the optimum print results. If the quality from the self test page is not acceptable, replace the ink cartridge. Additional Troubleshooting information is located in the Users Manual. If the print quality is still unacceptable, have the printer sent in for repair.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-36917 | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: My printout has incorrect margins. What should I do?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Error While Adding The paper may be loaded incorrectly. Make sure that the left and right edge guides are flush against both sides of the paper. Make sure the margins are set correctly in your software application (margins are not adjustable via the printer driver), and make sure you are not trying to print outside of the printable area of the page for your paper size (printable area of the page specifications are available in the User's Manual). Make sure the correct paper size is selected in the printer driver settings.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-36919 | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: My printout has lines running through it. What should I do?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Error While Adding This phenomena is called banding and may be caused by a clogged nozzle. Print the self test page by pressing the Load/Eject button while applying power to the printer. Check to see if white lines appear through your text. If the white lines are present, perform two cleaning cycles as described under the front cover of the printer; (COLOR Cleaning)-Press the PAUSE button, then ALT and Economy Condensed, or (BLACK Cleaning)-Press the PAUSE button, then ALT and Load/Eject. If the ink cartridge is new and the cleaning cycles did not correct the problem, print the default settings (Press Economy/Condensed and power the printer on) to ensure that "Autoline Feed" is off. If the "Autoline Feed" is off, and the test print still shows missing dots, have the printer sent in for repair. If the ink cartridge is old, and the cleaning cycles did not correct the problem, print the default settings to ensure that "Autoline Feed" is off. If the "Autoline Feed" is off, replace the ink cartridge. You may have to perform a few cleaning cycles after installing the new ink cartridge. Banding may also occur if the user turns the printer off by a power bar. If this is the case, the user may need to run up to 10 cleaning cycles to correct the problem. If the condition persists and it is visible in the demo page, the printer needs to be serviced. The Troubleshooting Guide located in Chapter Six of the User's Manual provides additional information.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-36920 | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: I am not getting either black or color on the self test page. What should I do?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Error While Adding Make sure the economy light on the printer is not on. This will ensure that the economy mode option is not selected. Using the instructions on the inside front cover of the printer as a guide, perform a black and then a color cleaning cycle; (COLOR Cleaning)-Press the PAUSE button, then ALT and Economy Condensed, or (BLACK Cleaning)-Press the PAUSE button, then ALT and Load/Eject. If this does not correct the problem, replace the appropriate ink cartridge.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-36923 | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: One of my colors is missing. What should I do?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Error While Adding Calibrate colors in the driver setup to the specific application or image. Set to Default and adjust from there. Perform self test (Press Load/Eject and power printer on) and check that seven colors are produced. If seven colors are not produced, print the default settings (Press Economy/Condensed and power printer on) to inspect dots. If test print shows missing dots, perform two cleaning cycles; (COLOR Cleaning)-Press the PAUSE Button, then, Alt and Economy Condensed 'OR' (BLACK Cleaning) Press the PAUSE, then, Alt and Load/Eject. Run the self test again to see if printing has improved. If the self test is still missing one of the four colors, try replacing the ink cartridge. If the condition persists, have the printer sent in for repair.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-45699 | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: What scanning resolution should I use to get the best results when I print the scanned image?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Error While Adding The Line Screen Frequency for EPSON Stylus printers can be calculated using the following formula:PrintingResolution____________3 X 1___2 = Line ScreenFrequencyYou should set the minimum scan resolution at 1/3rd of the desired print resolution. For example, for a print resolution of 720dpi, you will need to set the scan resolution to 240dpi or higher. If you are going to increase the size of the printed image then you should scan at a higher dpi resolution.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000ab0-shared | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Calibration doesn't seem to have any effect. What should I do?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Error While Adding Make sure that whenever you turn off the printer, you are always using the printer's power button. Never use an external switch, such as a power strip, surge protector, or power protector, to turn off the printer. If you turn the printer off using an external switch, calibration settings will not be saved.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-00004dc-shared | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Does Epson make drivers for Linux?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Error While Adding Linux drivers are available for some products. Visit the links below to see the drivers available for your product, as well as information about using an Epson product with Linux. Note: Epson does not provide support for Linux drivers. Linux drivers for Epson products Linux FAQs for Epson products(only available in English) Linux manuals for Epson products(only available in English)

coveo:metadata.title: faq-36918 | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: My product makes a grinding sound when I turn it on. What should I do?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Error While Adding Make sure the knob on the right side of the printer is not pushed in. If the knob Power the printer off and gently push the knob in then rotate the knob one turn and release the knob. Now try powering the printer back on. The sound should be gone.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-36921 | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Why won't my product do a self test?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Error While Adding Verify that the correct procedure for printing the self test has been performed. This problem is commonly caused by not holding down the required button long enough. Power on the printer while holding down the Load/Eject button until the data light comes on and the printer starts to load the paper. If the printhead is moving back and forth but there is no ink on the page, press pause, then turn the printer off. Using the instructions on the inside front cover of the printer as a guide, perform a two cleaning cycles; (COLOR Cleaning)-Press the PAUSE button, then ALT and Economy Condensed, or (BLACK Cleaning)-Press the PAUSE button, then ALT and Load/Eject. Try running the self test again. If self test still shows no ink on the page, contact the place of purchase for an exchange or send in the printer for service.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-45695 | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Why is there a loud noise when I print?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Error While Adding Make sure the knob on the right side of the printer is not pushed up against anything. That knob should be free from obstruction. You should be able to push it in and out and rotate it.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-000096d-shared | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Where can I get replacement parts or a service manual?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Error While Adding You can purchase replacement parts and service manuals from an Epson Authorized Customer Care Center or Epson parts distributor.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-00004b0-shared | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Does Epson have a program where I can donate my working electronic equipment?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Error While Adding Yes. Epson America, Inc. and Digitunity have joined together with the goal of helping those who are facing economic challenges or have a disability to gain access to today's technology. With your donation, you not only help the environment, but you also help change the lives of those who need these products the most.

coveo:metadata.title: faq-0000f4a-shared | SPT_C164001 | Epson Stylus Pro | Epson Stylus Series | Single Function Inkjet Printers | Printers | Support | Epson US
coveo:metadata.epson_question: Where do I find my product's serial number?
coveo:metadata.epson_answer: Error While Adding See our Finding Your Epson Product Serial Number page for details.
